project.title: Peptide mapping of SRSF7_RRM isoforms in murine P19 cells
project.projectDescription: Here we mapped peptides of mouse SRSF7_RRM purified from P19 cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Bacterial artificial chromosomes (BACs) harbouring the complete mouse SRSF7 gene with a C-terminal GFP-tag were inserted into the genome of murine P19 cells. SRSF7-GFP overexpressing clones were obtained by FACS. The SRSF7_RRM isoform was purified from cells by stringent IPs using an anti-SRSF7 antibody and SDS-PAGE. WT cells served as control. Coomassie stained protein bands corresponding to SRSF7_RRM were cut and digested with LysC and Chymotrypsin (Promega) overnight. Liquid chromatography/mass spectrometry (LC/MS) was performed on Thermo Scientific™ Q Exactive Plus equipped with an ultra-high performance liquid chromatography unit (Thermo Scientific Dionex Ultimate 3000) and a Nanospray Flex Ion-Source (Thermo Scientific). Peptides were loaded on a C18 reversed-phase precolumn (Thermo Scientific) followed by separation on a with 2.4 µm Reprosil C18 resin (Dr. Maisch GmbH) in-house packed picotip emitter tip (diameter 100 µm; 15 cm; New Objectives) using a linear gradient from 4% acetonitrile, 0.1% formic acid to 30% mobile solvent B (99% acetonitrile, 0.1% formic acid) for 90 min  followed by a second gradient to 60% solvent B for 15 min.  MS data were recorded by data dependent acquisition. The full MS scan range was 300 to 2000 m/z with resolution of 70000, and an automatic gain control (AGC) value of 3E6 total ion counts with a maximal ion injection time of 160 ms. Only higher charged ions (2+) were selected for MS/MS scans with a resolution of 17500, an isolation window of 2 m/z and an automatic gain control value set to E5 ions with a maximal ion injection time of 150 ms.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS Data were analyzed by Peaks7 Proteomics software (Bioinformatics solution) with a parent mass error tolerance of 8 ppm and a fragment mass error tolerance of 0.05 Da. Proteins were identified using protein database UniProtKB (Mus musculus, from 2018) and all putative SRSF7 isoforms were manually added (in total 52,551 entries). The enzyme specificity was set to Chymotrypsin and LysC or with maximal 3 mis-cleavages allowed. Oxidation of methionine (+15.99) and phosphorylation on serines, threonines and tyrosines (+79.97) and N-terminal acetylation (+42.01) were selected as variable modifications and carbamidomethylation (+57.02) as fixed modification on cysteines. False discovery rate (FDR) for the identification of peptides was 5%. Please also see Peaks7 output data (PDF).
